description: Japan's most important holiday, marked by family gatherings, temple visits, and traditional rituals. Many businesses and attractions are closed.
tips: Book accommodations early as hotels fill up quickly
Expect crowded temples and shrines for Hatsumode (first shrine visit)
Public transport operates on holiday schedules

event_name: Coming of Age Day (Seijin no Hi)
description: A national holiday celebrating young adults turning 20, with ceremonies at local shrines and public venues. Many wear traditional kimonos.
tips: Avoid major shrines during peak ceremony hours
Great opportunity to see traditional Japanese attire in public

description: A traditional event marking the start of spring, involving bean-throwing ceremonies to drive away evil spirits, held at temples and shrines.
tips: Arrive early for ceremonies as space is limited
Be prepared for loud chants and crowded temple grounds

event_name: National Foundation Day (Kenkoku Kinen no Hi)
description: A national holiday commemorating the founding of Japan. Many locals take the day off, but major tourist activities remain unaffected.
tips: Minimal impact on travel plans, but some government offices and businesses may be closed

description: One of the world's largest marathons, attracting thousands of runners and spectators. Major roads are closed for the event.
tips: Avoid driving or planning travel along the marathon route
Book hotels early as demand spikes
Check public transport updates for disruptions

description: A cultural event celebrating girls' health and happiness, with displays of ornamental dolls in homes and some public spaces.
tips: Look for doll displays in department stores or cultural centers
Little impact on travel plans

description: A national holiday marking the spring equinox, often associated with visiting family graves and appreciating nature. Parks may be busier.
tips: Expect more locals in parks and gardens
Good time for nature-related activities if crowds are manageable

description: The start of cherry blossom season in Tokyo, drawing large crowds to parks for hanami (flower viewing) picnics and events. Dates may vary slightly based on weather.
tips: Reserve spots for hanami in popular parks early
Visit during weekdays or early mornings to avoid peak crowds
Accommodation prices may rise due to high demand

description: A vibrant street parade celebrating Carnival with costumes, music, and dance, typically held before Lent.
tips: Expect road closures in central areas
Book accommodations early if staying near parade routes

description: A popular day for romantic getaways in Paris, with many couples visiting iconic landmarks and dining out.
tips: Reserve restaurants and romantic activities well in advance
Avoid peak times at popular spots like the Eiffel Tower

description: One of the biggest fashion events globally, attracting designers, models, and media to showcase Autumn/Winter collections.
tips: Expect increased traffic near event venues
Hotel prices may rise in upscale districts

description: A day of celebration before Lent, often marked by small events or traditional meals like crepes in Paris.
tips: Look for local crepe stands or events
Not a major tourist event but some cafes may be busier
